compiled_state.title: TEST_FILTER env var does not filter tests in test-pre-commit-test-gate.sh
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What happened

Attempted to run only rebase tests by setting TEST_FILTER env var:

```bash
TEST_FILTER="test_gate_rebase" timeout 120 bash tests/hooks/test-pre-commit-test-gate.sh
```

Expected: Only tests matching "test_gate_rebase" would run.
Actual: All 40 tests ran from the beginning (PASS: test_gate_blocked_missing_status, etc.), ignoring the filter.

## Root cause

The test file uses a custom `run_test` function but does not check for a TEST_FILTER environment variable. There is no built-in test selection mechanism in the bash test harness.

## Impact

- Cannot quickly iterate on specific tests — must wait for all preceding tests to complete
- Combined with the timeout issue (bug 092f-7643), this means late-registered tests are unreachable in normal Bash tool calls

## Suggested fix

Add TEST_FILTER support to the run_test function (or a shared test runner lib):

```bash
run_test() {
    local fn="\$1"
    if [[ -n "\${TEST_FILTER:-}" && "\$fn" != *"\$TEST_FILTER"* ]]; then
        return 0  # skip
    fi
    # existing test execution logic
}
```

comments.body: INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED: safeguard_approval | Behavioral code bug: TEST_FILTER not working. Fix requires editing test scripts (safeguard). Re-run in interactive session to approve.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 1 (BASIC). Missing TEST_FILTER support in test runner.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Intent-contradicting: TEST_FILTER was never implemented — this is a feature request, not a bug. No test file in the codebase supports TEST_FILTER.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
